question: Which deal was announced first in June 2026: Salesforce buying Fin, or SpaceX buying Cursor's maker?

openrouter:anthropic/claude-sonnet-5.answer: Salesforce's acquisition of Fin was announced first. Salesforce announced the deal on **June 15, 2026** ($3.6 billion) ([Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/salesforce-buy-fin-about-36-billion-2026-06-15/), [Salesforce press release](https://www.salesforce.com/news/press-releases/2026/06/15/salesforce-signs-definitive-agreement-to-acquire-fin/)), while SpaceX's acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor's maker) for $60 billion was announced the next day, on **June 16, 2026** ([Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/legal/transactional/spacex-buy-anysphere-60-billion-2026-06-16/)).

openrouter:openai/gpt-5.6-sol.answer:
**Salesforce buying Fin was announced first**, on **June 15, 2026**. The SpaceX deal to acquire Cursor’s maker, Anysphere, was announced on **June 16, 2026**—one day later.

Sources: [Salesforce](https://www.salesforce.com/news/press-releases/2026/06/15/salesforce-signs-definitive-agreement-to-acquire-fin/) · [Reuters on SpaceX–Anysphere](https://www.reuters.com/legal/transactional/spacex-buy-anysphere-60-billion-2026-06-16/)
